page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmc9i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized bare-metal instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture, powered by Intel Xeon 6982P-C processors operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. With a one-to-one ratio of physical cores to virtual CPUs and a single thread per core configuration, the instance eliminates hyperthreading overhead to ensure consistent and predictable compute performance.

The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. This allocation is tailored for compute-heavy workloads rather than memory-intensive applications. The instance contains no local storage and does not include integrated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage for data persistence. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 64 Gbps.

This server is designed for highly parallelized, compute-bound workloads that require dedicated physical cores and high clock speeds. The resource profile is suitable for high-performance computing, batch processing, intensive compilation, and large-scale simulation tasks where memory capacity is secondary to pure CPU execution speed. The bare-metal architecture provides direct hardware access, maximizing resource efficiency for demanding parallel processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmc9i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized bare-metal instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by Intel Xeon 6982P-C processors running at 3.2 GHz, the instance operates with a single thread per core, ensuring predictable execution without hyperthreading overhead. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 64 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, making it a highly specialized platform for raw CPU-bound tasks. This hardware profile is optimized for high-performance computing, batch processing, and parallelized simulation workloads requiring massive, dedicated compute capacity. The KVM-based bare-metal architecture provides direct hardware access, maximizing resource efficiency for demanding parallel processing tasks.
